name: Biscotti Di Prato (Little Almond Cookies)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Place both blanched and unblanched almonds on a cookie sheet and toast in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, until golden brown.
3. Grind 4 ounces of mixed blanched and unblanched almonds very fine. Cut the remaining toasted almonds into pieces.
4. In a mound of flour on a pasta board, make a well in the center. Add sugar and eggs to the well and mix thoroughly.
5. Incorporate salt, saffron, and baking soda into the mixture. Gradually add flour until all but about 2 tablespoons are incorporated.
6. Set aside leftover flour. Knead dough for 10-15 minutes, then add finely ground almonds and almond pieces. Knead for an additional 2-3 minutes.
7. Divide dough into 8 pieces. Shape each piece into a long, thin roll (about 3/4 inch in diameter) and place on a buttered and floured cookie sheet.
8. Beat egg white slightly in a bowl and brush the tops of rolls with it using a pastry brush. Place the baking sheet back in the preheated oven for 18-20 minutes.
9. Remove rolls from oven (they will expand sideways). Cut each roll at a 45-degree angle every 3/4 inch to achieve biscotti shape.
10. Place biscotti back in oven at 275°F for 35-45 minutes until very dry.
11. Serve and enjoy after 2 or 3 days when softened slightly; store in paper bag. For long-term storage, transfer to jar after a week.
